entries.decision: Supabase/PostgreSQL como plataforma de datos.
Precedencia KPIONE2 > POWER_APP > KPIONE1 audit-only.
Hechos diarios y semanales incrementales para Control Gestión.
Corrección de duplicación de rutas en v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2.
Runtime semanal en cg_mart.fact_cg_out_weekly_v2.
Fuente KPIONE2 productiva realineada a CUMPLIMIENTO_FRECUENCIA.xlsx / DB (KPIONE2.0).
Adoptar Git worktrees para fases riesgosas.
ChatGPT gobierna, Claude investiga y critica, Codex implementa y prueba.
La selección de modelo queda fuera del prompt operativo.
CG005N-Q preserva prefijos y anexa columnas de contrato al final.
Rollback exacto sustituido por rollback lógico signature-preserving.
Catálogo canónico compartido en cg005n_catalog_contract.py.
PostgreSQL 17 para laboratorio CG005N-Q.
Paquete CG005N-Q cerrado en feature branch antes de autorización Supabase.
Drift productivo previo clasificado NO_DRIFT.
No construir Skill/MCP de memoria por ahora.
Cambiar el equilibrio hacia mayor desarrollo funcional.
CG005N-Q PR #3 fue creado, marcado ready for review y mergeado a main como cambio code-only.
El bloqueo inicial por diff contra main local se clasificó como falso positivo; readiness debe comparar contra origin/main o base SHA congelado cuando main local esté desalineado.
Merge a main no autoriza Supabase DDL, SQL13 productivo, DB_URL_DDL, DB_URL_LOAD, loaders ni data load.
Limpieza Supabase debe entrar como discovery read-only con matriz KEEP/DEPRECATE/ARCHIVE/DROP_CANDIDATE antes de cualquier eliminación.
Supabase cleanup discovery primera pasada fue aceptada solo como inventario read-only, no como matriz decision-grade.
Claude se integró como auditor crítico complementario: no ejecuta; critica matriz, criterios, riesgos y puntos ciegos.
Segunda pasada Codex read-only clasificó 142/142 objetos: 136 KEEP_ACTIVE, 6 STOP_UNKNOWN, 0 candidatos DEPRECATE/ARCHIVE/DROP.
Supabase cleanup queda registrado como NO_DROP/NO_DEPRECATE/NO_ARCHIVE en research/SUPABASE_CLEANUP_NO_DROP_REGISTER_20260620.json.
La limpieza destructiva queda bloqueada por defecto; objetos LOW confidence o HIGH risk deben permanecer STOP_UNKNOWN hasta evidencia externa/runtime suficiente.
Tras cerrar CG005N-Q y registrar NO_DROP, el foco recomendado vuelve a desarrollo funcional visible antes de nuevas fases de limpieza.
Replace fine-grained administrative PRs with PRs by verifiable unit of value and promote canonical KERNEL files into governance/kernel/current.
context: The 010 series protected the project but produced excessive administrative PR fragmentation.
Documentos/KERNEL BASE FINAL was ignored by Git, so local KERNEL files were not a durable repo source of truth.
ChatGPT Project sources are useful context but can become stale against Git.
rules: Git governance/KERNEL is authoritative when tracked and merged.
ChatGPT Project sources are operational context.
If Git and ChatGPT Project sources diverge, alert the divergence.
Do not update KERNEL as a historical snapshot.
Do not continue 010 phases unless a real BLOCK remediation requires it.
entries.impact: 011 becomes the cutover from governance-by-many-PRs to compact execution doctrine.

entries.decision: Activate roadmap lock KPIONE_DB_TRANSITION_016_019_LOCK_V1 for the KPIONE DB transition sequence 016, 016A, 016B, 016C, 017A, 017B, 017C, 018, 019.
context: FAST_REFORM_016 is still active on branch lab/FAST_REFORM_016_kpione_monthly_db_precheck_read_only.
The sequence must not skip from 016 directly to apply, cleanup, retention, loader changes, app runtime changes, SQL changes, or destructive work.
Retention and destructive topics are deferred until after the post-apply trial.
rules: Only one phase is active: 016.
The only allowed next phase is 016A.
Deviation requires a directive amendment before work continues.
The legacy KPIONE database state is evidence of the previous runtime, not the migration authority for the new architecture.
The roadmap lock becomes authoritative only after the governance files are merged into Git.
No DB write, SQL execution, Supabase mutation, loader modification, app runtime modification, contracts 014/015 modification, git add, commit, or push is authorized by this ADR.
entries.impact: The KPIONE DB transition is now locked to a governed, auditable sequence before any future apply or trial work.

entries.decision: Amend KPIONE_DB_TRANSITION_016_019_LOCK_V1 to redefine 016A as legacy evidence closeout and governed transition readiness for separately authorized 016B.
context: PR #44 technically closed phase 016 and merged to main at 26fed014a2429811723ea35218a8619d9178efea.

016 evidence documents TABLE_STATE_CHANGED, historical rows 526022, current rows 45736, legacy_migration_authority=false, NO_SOURCE_SIGNAL not FRESH, exact overlap UNVERIFIABLE, and apply gate BLOCKED_FOR_IDEMPOTENCY_CONTRACT.
Hash reconciliation classified the prior cce9/a3cc mismatch as HASH_METHOD_MISMATCH_LINE_ENDINGS: Git blob LF bytes are canonical and CRLF working-tree bytes are not authority.
PR #45 review detected an 8 versus 9 authorized file count discrepancy; actual authorized scope is nine files including tests/test_sz_worktree_tooling.py.
rules: The canonical roadmap SHA method is SHA256_OF_CANONICAL_GIT_BLOB_BYTES_LF.
The previous SHA remains valid only for the pre-amendment directive.
016 is closed by PR #44.
016A is the registered current phase after this amendment.
The allowed next phase is 016B, but 016B is not authorized by this ADR.
No Supabase access, SQL execution, DB writes, apply, cutover, app runtime change, loader change, contracts 014/015 change, or legacy destructive action occurred or is authorized.
PR #45 review fix reconciles authorized repository file count to nine without productive behavior changes; 016B remains not authorized.
entries.impact: Governance may review the 016A amendment PR; any 016B design still requires a separate explicit authorization after the 016A PR is accepted.

entries.decision: Adopt Operating Model V2 for STOCK_ZERO governance: single authority surfaces, compact live state, risk-class governance, no standalone closeout phases, compressed future KPIONE roadmap, and Git/path authority instead of future manual directive-hash propagation.
context: Route B structural reform was accepted because governance overhead was displacing functional and operational progress.
PROJECT_KERNEL remains the constitution, PROJECT_STATE becomes live state only, PROJECT_LEDGER remains append-only durable decisions, and Git/GitHub hold repository history.
PROJECT_TECHNICAL_EVIDENCE is retained as a historical snapshot but deprecated as a live source.
rules: Do not manually duplicate facts that can be referenced from Git/GitHub or repository paths.

R1_REPO_SAFE requires PR + tests; R2_ARCHITECTURE requires ADR + PR + tests; R3_PRODUCTIVE requires authorization + plan + precheck + execution evidence + postcheck; R4_DESTRUCTIVE requires explicit authorization + impact analysis + backup + recovery plan.
Normal repository units do not require separate closeout phases once objective, tests, merge, and PROJECT_STATE update are complete.
Future KPIONE transition roadmap is compressed to 016B, 017, 018, and 019.
Future manual directive SHA propagation is retired; authority is repository path plus Git commit reference.
016B remains not authorized.
entries.impact: Governance is reduced to live state, durable decisions, directive gates, and Git/PR history; 016B may only begin after separate explicit authorization.

entries.decision: Select Route B photo exports as the future productive KPIONE source authority and use the source-photo-row to immutable event-photo staging-row contract with content-addressed source versions, explicit correction supersession, and derived event/day-presence outputs.

route_b.delivery_boundary: controlled input folder containing immutable source files

selected_contract.persistence_boundary: new_versioned_staging_boundary using batch, batch-file, and event-photo staging objects from the 014F design
selected_contract.legacy_authority: cg_raw.kpione2_raw and previous legacy state remain compatibility/history evidence only

identity_hierarchy: business_event: event_id with sp_item_id and event_stable_hash stability checks
day_presence: fecha + location_key + cliente_norm, derived as binary presence

idempotency_rules.corrected_file: new_source_version with explicit supersession before active projection changes
idempotency_rules.removed_rows: supersession with tombstone/exclusion in active projection; immutable rows remain
idempotency_rules.resumed_ingestion: update only if semantic plan and source versions are unchanged
idempotency_rules.duplicate_rows_inside_one_source: no_op for exact duplicate active records, with duplicate provenance retained
idempotency_rules.same_event_multiple_source_files: no_op for same photo hash, quarantine for event_stable_hash conflicts
idempotency_rules.concurrent_or_overlapping_runs: rejection unless same semantic plan and same source versions

correction_replay_model.source_versioning: source_file_sha256 identifies immutable content versions; declared batch coverage groups them
correction_replay_model.supersession: corrections activate only through declared supersession for the covered period
correction_replay_model.deletion_semantics: removed rows become active-projection tombstones or exclusions, not destructive deletes
correction_replay_model.rebuild_boundary: event rows, day presence and weekly metrics rebuild from the active source-version set
correction_replay_model.rollback_or_isolation: inactive/quarantined/rolled-back batches do not feed downstream projections

rejected_alternatives: legacy table state as identity authority
Route A operational workbook as future ingestion authority
permanent dual-source authority between Route A and Route B
filename or latest download as source identity
source row number alone as business identity
photo row counted directly as visit or daily presence
constraints_and_known_limitations: No DB, SQL, apply, cutover, loader, or app change is authorized by 016B.
Future 017 must implement runner behavior against this contract before any productive authorization.
Legacy overlap remains unverifiable and is intentionally not used as the migration authority.
implications_for_017: 017 is the next permitted unit but remains unauthorized.

017 must implement a folder-based Route B runner that scans a configured input folder, identifies candidate photo-excel-admin_*.xlsx files, computes SHA256 before processing, registers batch and file versions, validates sheet/schema, stages photo rows immutably, activates only complete validated runs, quarantines malformed or partial files, avoids reprocessing identical content, and derives events/day presence from active source versions.
The input folder path is runtime configuration and provenance, not identity authority.
Migration mistakes must be corrected through versioned staging, replay, supersession, rehearsal, and trial rather than by retaining Route A as future authority.
017 must not use cg_raw.kpione2_raw, filename, or latest-download semantics as identity authority.

entries.decision: Implement the independent folder-based KPIONE Route B runner and select it as ready for a separately authorized 018 productive apply after a successful ephemeral local PostgreSQL rehearsal.
rules: scripts/run_kpione_route_b_ingestion_v1.py is the independent Route B ingestion boundary; Route A remains history and parity evidence only.
The lifecycle is DISCOVERED, VALIDATING, VALIDATED, STAGING, STAGED, ACTIVE, QUARANTINED, SUPERSEDED, ROLLED_BACK, or FAILED.
Activation occurs only at the final transaction gate after complete file registration, validation, immutable staging, conflict checks, and deterministic projection readiness.

Every discovered file is classified independently; active source versions are skipped and only new content versions enter the semantic activation plan, so mixed folders cannot return early on a single known hash.
All apply, supersession, and rollback decisions are serialized with the stable KPIONE_ROUTE_B_V1 PostgreSQL transaction advisory lock before active-version or coverage queries.

The database enforces at most one ACTIVE batch for any overlapping daterange through the repeatable kpione_one_active_coverage_v1 GiST exclusion constraint; together with the advisory lock this prevents duplicate active source versions under concurrent execution.
Corrections require an explicit active predecessor and atomically supersede it; immutable child rows are preserved.
Rollback is logical: the correction becomes ROLLED_BACK, its eligible predecessor is restored, and views resolve from ACTIVE batches without deleting staging evidence.
Workbook errors detected before a batch exists are FAILED_BEFORE_REGISTRATION; QUARANTINED and FAILED remain registered-batch lifecycle states.
Local apply requires DB_URL_CODEX_LOCAL and a loopback PostgreSQL host; Supabase, remote hosts, generic DB environment names, and remote SSL metadata fail closed.

The rehearsal covered clean schema, first ingest, same-file and renamed-content no-ops, malformed and event-conflict rejection, correction detection, supersession, removed-row projection, rollback, deterministic replay, and projection count equality.
018_PRODUCTIVE_APPLY is next permitted but remains NOT_AUTHORIZED.
entries.impact: Route B is technically ready for 018 planning; no productive authority, apply, cutover, or destructive action is granted.

deprecated_patterns.replacement: Satisfacer el contrato físico o detener

deprecated_patterns.pattern: Asumir variables compartidas entre PowerShell y Codex
deprecated_patterns.replacement: Configurar y validar en el proceso ejecutor

deprecated_patterns.pattern: Aceptar tests sintéticos como prueba suficiente de DDL
Guardar toda la conversación como memoria
deprecated_patterns.replacement: Promover solo decisiones, reglas y evidencia útil
